description: Prevention hardening for parallel-slot loop conflicts and merge-back cascades. Closes the path-drift bug that caused the mw-datalake incident, adds halt-on-consecutive-merge-fail, implicit-overlap detection for shared-infra files (Cargo.lock / uv.lock / package-lock.json / go.sum etc.), cross-wave file affinity, and stale ephemeral branch hygiene.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test` (scoped per iteration; full at REVIEW-001)
No breaking changes to existing public APIs unless explicitly required by the task description
All new fields on serde-deserialized structs use `#[serde(default)]` so older configs Just Work
Reuse existing helpers (id_body_matches_prefix, ensure_worktree, ephemeral_slot_branch, read_project_config, list_other_roots) — do not fork parallel implementations
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Recomputing slot worktree paths inside merge-back code (the bug we are fixing)
New parallel matchers for task-id prefixes — reuse id_body_matches_prefix from selection.rs
Silent loop continuation past a merge-back failure — must increment counter and surface diagnostics
Direct edits to tasks/*.json or tasks.db from production code paths

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` accepts `slot_paths: &[PathBuf]` and uses `&slot_paths[0]` for all slot-0 git operations
Positive: WaveParams (or equivalent) plumbing in engine.rs:~1430 passes the actual paths returned by `ensure_slot_worktrees`
Negative: No call to `compute_slot_worktree_path(_, _, 0)` remains inside `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver`

Unit test: `test_merge_slot_branches_succeeds_when_invoked_from_inside_slot0_worktree` — fixture creates a tempdir, runs git ops with `current_dir(slot0_path)` such that `compute_slot_worktree_path` would return a non-existent path; merge-back succeeds without ENOENT

Unit test: existing `test_merge_slot_branches_merges_disjoint_changes` (worktree.rs:2126) and `test_merge_slot_branches_recovers_from_partial_failure` (line 2184) updated to pass slot_paths and pass without behavior change

Known-bad: a fix that simply guards `if !slot0_path.exists()` would mask the bug but still fail when slot 0's path differs validly (e.g. testing scenarios). Test fixture must use a topology where the recomputed path is genuinely wrong
Failure mode: if `slot_paths.len() < num_slots`, return early with a clear error rather than panicking on index access
CONTRACT: WaveParams field name and type match what engine.rs:2840 / dispatch_slot_wave already produces
userStories.description:
Fix the path-drift bug that caused the mw-datalake ENOENT cascade. `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` (worktree.rs:778) currently calls `compute_slot_worktree_path(project_root, branch_name, 0)` at line 789 to derive slot 0's path. But `ensure_slot_worktrees` (line 599) gets slot 0's actual path via `ensure_worktree`, which returns `project_root` itself when the loop runs from inside the matching worktree — a different path than the recomputed one. Result: `Command::current_dir(&slot0_path)` ENOENT.

DO: Add a `slot_paths: &[PathBuf]` parameter to `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` and use `&slot_paths[0]` instead of recomputing. Thread through `WaveParams` from engine.rs:1430. Update the existing test fixtures (worktree.rs:2126, 2184) to pass slot_paths.

DO NOT: Touch `cleanup_slot_worktrees` (worktree.rs:1095) — it runs from outside any worktree where `compute_slot_worktree_path` is correct. Don't add a new path resolver trait or DI seam — straightforward parameter passing is the contract.

Edge cases:
- Loop runs from inside slot 0's main worktree: paths[0] equals project_root. Correct.
- Loop runs from outside any worktree: paths[0] equals `compute_slot_worktree_path(project_root, branch, 0)`. Correct.
- `num_slots <= 1`: function returns early at line 785; no change.

Known-bad to guard against: Re-deriving slot 0's path inside the merge-back function 'just to be safe' — that's exactly the bug we are removing.
edgeCases: Loop running from inside main worktree: slot_paths[0] == project_root
Loop running from main repo (outside any worktree): slot_paths[0] == compute_slot_worktree_path
num_slots == 1: function returns early; no path used

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return MergeOutcomes with PreResolver failures for missing slots; do not panic

userStories.notes: Existing helpers to reuse: ensure_slot_worktrees (worktree.rs:573) returns the Vec<PathBuf>, ephemeral_slot_branch (line 540) for branch names. Test fixture pattern: setup_conflicting_slot1 at worktree.rs:2485 — extend or copy. Learning [2010]: real-git tempdir is the standard fixture pattern. Learning [1804]: slot 0 reuses base branch; slots 1+ get ephemeral branches.
qualityDimensions: Single source of truth for slot path resolution within a wave (paths flow from ensure_slot_worktrees through WaveParams to merge-back)
No silent path-drift: the test that reproduces the mw-datalake ENOENT must fail pre-fix and pass post-fix
Function signature change is the smallest possible surface area — no trait, no resolver indirection
userStories.title: Pass slot worktree paths into merge-back instead of recomputing

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: ProjectConfig has `merge_fail_halt_threshold: u32` field, default 2 via `#[serde(default = "default_merge_fail_halt_threshold")]` and matching update to `Default for ProjectConfig`
Positive: WaveOutcome carries `failed_merges: Vec<usize>` populated from `outcomes.failed_slots` at the merge call site
Positive: IterationContext has `consecutive_merge_fail_waves: u32` (default 0)

Positive: Wave-loop boundary executes the 5-step reset/halt-check contract in order: reset → increment → check → diagnostics → break. Halt diagnostic message includes each failed slot's ephemeral branch name (use `ephemeral_slot_branch(branch, N)`)
Positive: Successful wave (failed_merges empty) resets the consecutive counter to 0
Negative: A halted run must NOT leave any task in `in_progress` for the failed slots — they must be reset to `todo` before the break
Negative: A failed wave must NOT skip the reset just because the threshold isn't reached; reset is unconditional, halt is conditional
Unit test: WaveOutcome.failed_merges is populated correctly when resolver returns failed_slots; empty otherwise
Unit test: ctx.consecutive_merge_fail_waves increments on failure; resets to 0 on a fully-successful wave
Integration test: 2-slot loop with one forced merge-back failure (e.g. resolver timeout = 1s on a deliberate conflict) — loop continues and the failed slot's task is back to `todo`
Integration test: 2-slot loop with two consecutive forced merge-back failures — loop aborts on the second wave with cited branch names; failed slots' tasks are reset
Integration test: same as above with `merge_fail_halt_threshold: 0` — loop never aborts (legacy behavior preserved)
Known-bad: an implementation that increments the counter BEFORE the reset would leave the DB inconsistent on halt — test must verify reset happens even when threshold is reached
Failure mode: if reset itself fails for a task, log the failure but continue with remaining failed slots; threshold check still runs on the original failed_merges count
CONTRACT: ephemeral_slot_branch(branch, N) is the canonical name source — do not construct branch names with format!() inline
userStories.description:
When merge-back fails, the wave loop currently logs a warning and launches the next wave anyway, which let the mw-datalake incident cascade across many iterations. Add a `consecutive_merge_fail_waves` counter and a `merge_fail_halt_threshold` config (default 2 — single failures are recoverable, two in a row indicate a cascade).

DO: Add `merge_fail_halt_threshold: u32` to ProjectConfig (default 2 via #[serde(default)]). Add `failed_merges: Vec<usize>` to WaveOutcome, populated from outcomes.failed_slots at engine.rs:1436. Add `consecutive_merge_fail_waves: u32` to IterationContext (engine.rs:~256). At the wave-loop boundary (engine.rs:~3149), execute the reset/halt-check contract IN ORDER: (1) reset every failed slot's claimed task to `todo` via the reset path used at engine.rs:3464-3482, drain those task IDs from ctx.pending_slot_tasks (mirror engine.rs:1269); (2) increment the consecutive counter; (3) compare to threshold; (4) emit per-slot ephemeral branch names; (5) break if threshold reached. On a successful wave merge-back, reset the counter to 0.

DO NOT: Halt on a single merge-back failure (default threshold is 2). Do NOT skip the task-reset step before halting — halted runs must leave the DB in a re-runnable state. Do NOT change the resolver's own behavior (timeout, prompt, model).

Threshold semantics: 0 = never halt (legacy behavior); 1 = halt on any failure; 2 = halt after 2 consecutive (default).
edgeCases: First wave fails: counter goes 0 → 1; threshold=2 means continue
Two consecutive fails: counter goes 0 → 1 → 2; halt at second wave
Fail then success then fail: counter 0 → 1 → 0 → 1; no halt
merge_fail_halt_threshold == 0: never halts regardless of counter
All slots succeed merge-back but one slot's task was reset earlier in the wave: failed_merges is empty; counter resets to 0

failureModes.cause: Reset path fails for one of the failed-slot tasks (DB locked, etc.)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log the per-task failure, continue resetting remaining failed-slot tasks, then proceed with threshold check

failureModes.cause: Threshold reached but no slots actually failed (impossible, but defensive)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Continue normally; halt requires non-empty failed_merges

userStories.notes: Reset path lives at engine.rs:3464-3482 (post-loop cleanup). Factor into a small helper if not already; FEAT-002 calls it from both the wave-loop boundary AND the existing post-loop site. pending_slot_tasks drain mirror at engine.rs:1269. Helper for branch names: ephemeral_slot_branch (worktree.rs:540). Learning [1870]: ephemeral branch naming convention is `{branch}-slot-{N}`.
qualityDimensions: Reset/halt ordering is contractual — failed-slot tasks are always reset to `todo` before any halt
Threshold default of 2 catches cascades but tolerates transient single failures
Diagnostics name the specific ephemeral branches so operators know where un-merged commits live
Counter resets correctly on success — a single failure followed by a success does not lower the next halt threshold
userStories.title: Halt loop after consecutive merge-back failures

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `IMPLICIT_OVERLAP_FILES` const exists in selection.rs with the documented Rust/Python/JS/Go basenames
Positive: `BUILDY_TASK_PREFIXES` const exists; doc-comment notes it is a superset of SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES
Positive: `id_has_buildy_prefix` reuses `id_body_matches_prefix` (no parallel matcher)
Positive: ProjectConfig has `implicit_overlap_files: Vec<String>` with `#[serde(default)]`
Positive: PrdFile has `implicit_overlap_files: Option<Vec<String>>` with `#[serde(default)]`
Positive: Task struct has `claimsSharedInfra: Option<bool>` with `#[serde(default)]`; JSON parse round-trips correctly
Positive: `select_parallel_group` adds `__shared_infra__` to used_files when path-detection (a), prefix-heuristic (b), or explicit override (c) fires
Negative: TEST/CLARIFY/DOCS/MILESTONE/CODE-REVIEW/HUMAN-REVIEW tasks must NOT claim the slot via the buildy heuristic
Negative: A task's persisted touchesFiles must NOT be mutated by the implicit-overlap logic (synthetic claim is in-memory only)
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_two_feats_compete_for_infra_slot` — disjoint touchesFiles, both FEAT, returned group has at most one
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_feat_plus_test_both_parallelize` — disjoint files, FEAT and TEST both run
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_feat_plus_clarify_both_parallelize`
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_explicit_cargo_lock_listed_takes_slot` — TEST that explicitly lists Cargo.lock claims via path (a)
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_claims_shared_infra_true_forces_claim` — TEST with claimsSharedInfra:true claims
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_claims_shared_infra_false_skips_buildy_heuristic` — FEAT with claimsSharedInfra:false does NOT claim
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_python_uv_lock_serializes_two_feats` — basename matching works for non-Rust ecosystems
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_project_config_extends_implicit_list`
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_prd_override_extends_project_implicit_list`
Unit test: `test_id_has_buildy_prefix_matches_uuid_prefixed_ids` — `cbd7d081-FEAT-001` matches; `cbd7d081-TEST-001` does not; `cbd7d081-FEATURE-001` does not (the trailing-dash boundary is enforced)
Known-bad: a task with id `cbd7d081-FEAT-001` and bare `id.starts_with("FEAT")` would NOT match (UUID prefix breaks naive check). Test must use real UUID-prefixed ids
Failure mode: if config files contain malformed `implicit_overlap_files` (non-string entries), serde rejects with a clear error; default falls back to baseline list
CONTRACT: Task struct field `claimsSharedInfra` matches camelCase JSON via existing serde rename convention (verify against existing field naming in src/models/task.rs)
userStories.description:
Two parallel `cargo build` runs regenerated `Cargo.lock` differently in the mw-datalake incident — neither task listed `Cargo.lock` in `touchesFiles` so the existing file-overlap check did not fire. Add a default list of build-system files (Rust / Python / JS / Go) that are treated as a single shared-infra slot, plus a buildy-task-type heuristic that auto-claims the slot for FEAT/REFACTOR/CODE-FIX/WIRE-FIX/IMPL-FIX tasks.

DO: Define `pub(crate) const IMPLICIT_OVERLAP_FILES: &[&str]` in selection.rs covering Rust (Cargo.lock, Cargo.toml), Python (uv.lock, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, requirements.lock, Pipfile, Pipfile.lock, poetry.lock), JS (package.json, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, npm-shrinkwrap.json, bun.lockb), Go (go.mod, go.sum, go.work, go.work.sum). Match by basename across full paths. Define `BUILDY_TASK_PREFIXES: &[&str] = &["FEAT", "REFACTOR", "REFACTOR-N", "CODE-FIX", "WIRE-FIX", "IMPL-FIX"]` — a superset of `SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES` (selection.rs:42). Add helper `fn id_has_buildy_prefix(id: &str) -> bool` that REUSES the existing `id_body_matches_prefix` (selection.rs:51) so PRD-UUID-prefixed ids like `cbd7d081-FEAT-001` match correctly. Add `implicit_overlap_files: Vec<String>` to ProjectConfig (project_config.rs) and `implicit_overlap_files: Option<Vec<String>>` to PrdFile (init/parse.rs:150) — both with `#[serde(default)]`. Add `claimsSharedInfra: Option<bool>` to the Task struct (and the JSON parse path) as a per-task override.

In `select_parallel_group` (selection.rs:436), introduce a synthetic claim token `"__shared_infra__"`. For each candidate: (a) if any file in `candidate.files` has a basename in the implicit-set union, add `__shared_infra__` plus those files to its claim. (b) Else if `id_has_buildy_prefix(candidate.task.id)` AND `claimsSharedInfra != Some(false)`, add `__shared_infra__`. (c) `claimsSharedInfra: Some(true)` forces the claim regardless of (a)/(b). The greedy accept/defer logic is unchanged otherwise.

DO NOT: Match implicit-overlap files by full path (basename only). Do NOT introduce a second prefix-matcher — reuse `id_body_matches_prefix`. Do NOT make the heuristic apply to TEST/CLARIFY/DOCS/MILESTONE/CODE-REVIEW/HUMAN-REVIEW tasks (they parallelize freely). Do NOT auto-add lockfiles to a task's persisted touchesFiles — the synthetic claim lives in the in-memory used_files set only.

`claimsSharedInfra` semantics: `Some(false)` overrides BOTH (a) and (b) — full opt-out; `Some(true)` forces claim regardless of prefix; `None` falls through to (a) ∨ (b).
edgeCases: Task lists `path/to/sub/Cargo.lock`: basename match fires
Task lists `Cargo.lockfoo`: no match (basename differs)
FEAT task with empty touchesFiles: still claims the slot via heuristic (most common cargo-build case)
Two TESTs with disjoint touchesFiles, neither lists a lockfile: both run
claimsSharedInfra:true on a TEST: claims the slot
Project config implicit_overlap_files = ["custom.txt"]: union with baseline; tasks touching custom.txt also serialize
PRD override implicit_overlap_files = ["custom2.txt"]: union with project + baseline

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Defaults to None via #[serde(default)] — heuristic decides
Defaults to empty Vec — only baseline fires

userStories.notes: Reuse: id_body_matches_prefix at selection.rs:51, SPAWNED_FIXUP_PREFIXES at line 42, build_scored_candidates at line 164. Test fixture base: src/commands/next/tests.rs around line 500 has parallel-group tests; extend that pattern. PrdFile struct in src/commands/init/parse.rs:150. Task struct in src/models/task.rs (verify camelCase serde convention). Learning [1855]: select_parallel_group signature evolved — verify current signature in source rather than trust learning. Learning [1755]: greedy algorithm; [1756]: empty touchesFiles always parallelizable.
qualityDimensions: Heuristic catches the mw-datalake incident pattern (two FEATs running cargo without listing Cargo.lock)
Non-buildy task types (TEST/CLARIFY/DOCS) parallelize freely — no over-serialization
Per-task `claimsSharedInfra` escape hatch is honored in both directions (force-claim and force-skip)
Basename matching means monorepos with sub-projects all benefit
Reuses existing prefix matcher — no new boundary-handling bugs
userStories.title: Implicit-overlap shared-infra files with buildy-task heuristic

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `list_unmerged_branch_files` exists in worktree.rs and runs `git diff --name-only {base}...{ephemeral}` from the given repo path
Positive: Returns empty Vec (not error) when the ephemeral branch does not exist (cleanly-recovered loop)
Positive: `select_parallel_group` augments `used_files` with un-merged files from every `{branch}-slot-N` branch present in the worktree list
Positive: SelectionResult has new `ephemeral_block_diagnostics` field (or out-param) carrying `(candidate_id, blocking_branches)` pairs when a candidate is exclusively blocked
Positive: Engine layer treats an empty-group-with-diagnostics result identically to a wave with `failed_merges` non-empty (increment counter, run FEAT-002 reset/halt contract)
Negative: Must NOT be invoked from inside a slot worker thread; doc-comment on the helper enforces between-waves usage
Negative: Must NOT call `git diff` once per candidate — cache the per-branch file list per wave
Unit test: `test_list_unmerged_branch_files_returns_empty_for_missing_branch`
Unit test: `test_list_unmerged_branch_files_returns_diff_for_real_branch` — set up tempdir with two commits on an ephemeral branch not in base, assert the touched files are returned
Unit test: `test_parallel_group_defers_when_ephemeral_branch_has_unmerged_file` — fixture has an ephemeral branch with `src/foo.rs` modified; candidate touching `src/foo.rs` is deferred

Unit test: `test_parallel_group_deadlock_guard_reports_when_all_candidates_blocked_by_ephemeral` — fixture where every eligible candidate is blocked solely by ephemeral overlap; SelectionResult.ephemeral_block_diagnostics is non-empty and group is empty
Integration test: 2-slot loop with manually-injected un-merged ephemeral branch; next wave's selection respects the cross-wave overlay; loop halts via the deadlock guard if every candidate is blocked
Known-bad: a fix that silently returns an empty group on the deadlock case lets the loop spin until stale-iteration abort — must instead surface diagnostics and halt with named branches
Failure mode: if `git diff` itself fails (corrupted ref etc.), log error and treat as empty file list — selection continues without the cross-wave overlay rather than crashing the loop
CONTRACT: ephemeral branch enumeration uses the same `{branch}-slot-N` shape produced by `ephemeral_slot_branch` (worktree.rs:540)
userStories.description:
Even with FEAT-002's halt, a manually-recovered or `merge_fail_halt_threshold: 0` setup can have transient un-merged ephemeral commits between waves. `select_parallel_group` only sees DB state, not git ephemeral-branch state, so it can pick a task that conflicts with un-merged work.

DO: Add `pub(crate) fn list_unmerged_branch_files(repo_path: &Path, base_branch: &str, ephemeral_branch: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>` in worktree.rs that runs `git diff --name-only {base}...{ephemeral}` and returns the file list (empty Vec if branch missing). In `select_parallel_group`, before the greedy pass, enumerate `{branch}-slot-N` ephemeral branches via the existing `list_other_roots` (worktree.rs:85) helper and add their un-merged files to `used_files` as synthetic claims.

Add a deadlock guard: track in select_parallel_group whether any candidate was deferred SOLELY due to ephemeral-overlap (not by formal dependsOn, soft-dep, or standard touchesFiles overlap). When the returned group is empty AND at least one such exclusively-blocked candidate exists, return diagnostics via `SelectionResult::ephemeral_block_diagnostics` (new field). Engine treats this as equivalent to a merge-fail wave: increment `consecutive_merge_fail_waves`, run the FEAT-002 reset/halt-check contract.

DO: Run `list_unmerged_branch_files` only on the main thread between waves — slot worker threads from the prior wave must have joined (engine.rs:~1381 join discipline). DO NOT invoke from inside a slot thread.

DO NOT: Make this a hot-path query for every candidate — call it once per ephemeral branch per wave and cache the resulting file set. DO NOT silently halt without diagnostics — the deadlock guard must surface the blocking branch names to the operator.
edgeCases: No ephemeral branches present: helper returns empty; select_parallel_group behavior unchanged
Ephemeral branch is fully merged into base: git diff returns empty file list
All candidates blocked solely by ephemeral overlap: deadlock guard fires
Some candidates blocked by ephemeral, others by standard overlap: deadlock guard does NOT fire (mixed-cause is not a deadlock)
Ephemeral branch on a different fork point than current base: git diff still works (it uses merge-base internally)

failureModes.cause: git diff fails (corrupted ref, missing branch mid-iteration)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log warning, treat that branch's contribution as empty, continue selection

failureModes.cause: Deadlock guard fires repeatedly across waves
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Each occurrence increments consecutive_merge_fail_waves; threshold reached → halt

userStories.notes: Existing helpers: list_other_roots (worktree.rs:85) for enumerating worktrees, ephemeral_slot_branch for canonical naming. select_parallel_group at selection.rs:436. SelectionResult struct around selection.rs:145. Concurrency: between-waves only — slot threads have already joined (engine.rs:~1381). Run-id race: two concurrent loops on the same branch are out of scope (existing soft-dep guard same scope).
qualityDimensions: Cross-wave overlap is invisible to candidates by default — diagnostics surface only when it actually blocks selection
Deadlock guard converts a silent infinite-spin into the same visible halt operators understand from FEAT-002
Caching: one git diff per ephemeral branch per wave (not per candidate)
Fail-soft on git errors: a corrupted ref does not crash the whole loop
userStories.title: Cross-wave file affinity from un-merged ephemeral branches with deadlock guard

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots` exists in worktree.rs with the documented four-case classification
Positive: Called from engine.rs immediately before `ensure_slot_worktrees`
Positive: Orphan branches (worktree dir missing entirely) are safely deleted
Positive: Already-merged clean ephemeral branches and worktrees are cleaned up
Positive: Un-merged clean ephemeral aborts startup when halt_threshold > 0; warning lists branch name and un-merged commit subjects
Positive: Dirty worktree always aborts startup with operator guidance — never auto-cleaned
Negative: Must NOT delete a branch in case 3 even when halt_threshold == 0
Negative: Must NOT use `git diff` for the ancestor check — use `git merge-base --is-ancestor`
Unit test: `test_reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots_deletes_orphan_branch` — branch exists, worktree dir missing → branch deleted
Unit test: `test_reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots_deletes_clean_merged` — clean worktree, ephemeral is ancestor → both deleted
Unit test: `test_reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots_aborts_on_unmerged_when_halt_enabled` — halt_threshold=2, clean unmerged → returns Err
Unit test: `test_reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots_warns_on_unmerged_when_halt_disabled` — halt_threshold=0, clean unmerged → returns Ok with warning logged, branch preserved
Unit test: `test_reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots_aborts_on_dirty_worktree` — dirty worktree → returns Err regardless of halt_threshold

Known-bad: an implementation using `git diff base...ephemeral` instead of `merge-base --is-ancestor` will misclassify after the base branch advances (false positive on un-merged) — test must include a topology where base advanced after the ephemeral was forked
Failure mode: `git branch --list` returns empty: function returns Ok early, no work to do
Failure mode: a single branch fails classification (e.g. mid-deletion by another process): log per-branch error, continue with remaining branches, do not abort the entire reconciliation pass
CONTRACT: branch-list pattern matches `{branch}-slot-*` exactly — same shape produced by `ephemeral_slot_branch` (worktree.rs:540)
userStories.description:
If a prior loop crashed after `ensure_slot_worktrees` but before `cleanup_slot_worktrees`, stale `{branch}-slot-N` branches remain. Today there is no startup reconciliation — the next loop's `ensure_slot_worktrees` may collide or silently reuse stale state.

DO: Add `pub(crate) fn reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots(project_root: &Path, branch_name: &str, halt_threshold: u32)` in worktree.rs. Called from engine.rs near where `ensure_slot_worktrees` is invoked (~line 2840), BEFORE `ensure_slot_worktrees` runs. List all branches matching `{branch}-slot-*` via `git branch --list`. For each, classify into one of four cases via `git merge-base --is-ancestor {ephemeral} {base_branch}`, worktree directory existence (`compute_slot_worktree_path`), and `git status --porcelain` for dirtiness:

  1. **Orphan branch (worktree missing entirely)** — safe-delete via `git branch -D {ephemeral}`.
  2. **Clean worktree, ephemeral is ancestor of base** (already merged) — delete branch + worktree.
  3. **Clean worktree, ephemeral NOT ancestor** (un-merged commits) — emit warning naming the branch and listing un-merged commit subjects. If `halt_threshold > 0`, abort startup with operator guidance. Do NOT silently delete.
  4. **Dirty worktree** — abort startup with operator guidance (do not touch).

DO NOT: Use `git diff` for the ancestor check — `merge-base --is-ancestor` is the correct primitive (handles base-branch advancement). DO NOT auto-delete in case 3 even when `halt_threshold = 0` — case 3 is operator-actionable; deletion would silently lose work.
edgeCases: No matching branches: Ok early-return
Branch exists but worktree dir was manually deleted: case 1 (orphan)
Branch is fully merged: case 2
Operator's manual recovery left an un-merged ephemeral with halt_threshold=0: case 3, warn, preserve
Operator left WIP in slot 1's worktree: case 4, abort

failureModes.cause: git branch -D fails for an orphan (concurrent process)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log per-branch error, continue with remaining branches
Treat as case 3 (un-merged) — fail-safe to operator review

userStories.notes: Existing helpers: ephemeral_slot_branch (worktree.rs:540), compute_slot_worktree_path (line 549), cleanup_slot_worktrees (line 1095) for the actual deletion mechanics. Use cleanup_slot_worktrees' branch-deletion code path for cases 1 and 2. userStories.description:
Review all implementation in this PRD for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability. Worktree.rs has historical pressure toward deep match nesting (per learning [2003]); the new code in FEAT-001/004/005 must not perpetuate that.

DO: Check for duplicated logic across the new helpers (list_unmerged_branch_files, reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots, the merge-back path-passing). Verify FEAT-002's reset path is a single helper, not duplicated between the wave-loop boundary and the post-loop cleanup site. Check function lengths in worktree.rs — flag anything > ~80 lines for extraction.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches the deep-nesting drift before REVIEW-001 runs the full suite.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) — atomic DB+JSON sync. If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>`.
qualityDimensions: DRY across modules — single source for reset logic and ephemeral-branch enumeration
Single-responsibility functions — each helper does one git operation or one classification
Pattern consistency with existing worktree.rs helpers (return types, error wrapping, logging style)
Historical-debt-aware: avoid deepening match nesting that learning [2003] flagged
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Check every new function for error handling. Verify each new ProjectConfig / PrdFile / Task field is reachable from production code paths (grep for the field name). Verify the wave-loop boundary's reset/halt contract uses the actual helper from FEAT-002 (not a copy). Run full `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` — no scope filter. Check if CLAUDE.md needs an update for the new parallel-slot scheduling rules (likely YES — add a 'Parallel-slot scheduling' section documenting the implicit-overlap baseline, buildy heuristic, halt threshold default, and stale-branch hygiene).

DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — verify every new field is actually read by the consumer (grep for field name in the consumer module). DO NOT scope the test suite — this is THE place the full suite runs.

acceptanceCriteria: reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots body is under 80 lines
All FEAT-005 tests still pass (cargo test reconcile_stale_ephemeral)

Idempotency invariant preserved
userStories.description: FEAT-005's reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots in src/loop_engine/worktree.rs (lines 662-806) is ~145 body lines, well above the ~80-line target in REFACTOR-001 AC3. The function has 5 separable phases: (1) branch enumeration via up-front git worktree prune + git branch --list --format=%(refname:short) glob; (2) per-branch classification fold via match classify_ephemeral_branch populating unmerged + dirty_paths; (3) unmerged commit-subject diagnostics emission; (4) dirty-worktree abort; (5) halt-threshold abort for unmerged. Proposed extraction: enumerate_stale_ephemeral_branches helper for phase 1, classify_stale_branches helper for phase 2 returning (unmerged, dirty_paths). Phases 4-5 stay inline at the function tail. Resulting body should land at ~50 lines. No behavior change — all four EphemeralCase arms keep identical semantics; idempotency invariant preserved.
userStories.title: Extract helpers from reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots to meet 80-line target
